meta.description: All mini-games, all the time. No flash missions, no standing missions. Tests whether focused interactive challenges create a better experience than the standard diverse event mix.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching. This feels like we're already playing standing missions instead of the actual game.
marcus.internal_thought: It's minute 3 and we're already voting on whether someone grabbed their phone—this is exactly the kind of chaos that derails focus, and we haven't even started the real game yet.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, was Pat actually on their phone? I wasn't even looking! This is so fun, we're doing this already?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is happening, we're already voting on stuff and it's EARLY and everyone's still fresh — this night is going to be insane.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, hold up — I didn't catch anyone on their phone. This is literally the first event and we're three minutes in. Are we really doing this right now?
pat.internal_thought: This feels like a gotcha attempt on me specifically, and it's weird — I was literally just looking around to manage the room, not checking my phone, and now I'm being called out on something that didn't happen. Also, that description about the AI 'watching' and 'knowing my weaknesses' is genuinely creepy and making me uncomfortable.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... probably legit. I saw Pat checking their phone like five minutes ago during the rules explanation.
river.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually check their phone or am I just agreeing because everyone's probably going to vote legit anyway and it's not worth the friction?

timeline.title: Celebrity Impression
timeline.description: First person to do a recognizable celebrity impression wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Nope. Not doing impressions right now. We're literally in setup—can we just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I was worried about—we haven't even started and the standing missions are already pulling focus. Also that 'AI knows your weaknesses' thing is genuinely unsettling and feels designed to mess with me.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — celebrity impressions, I'm here for this. Come on people, let's see what you got!
pat.internal_thought: This is way better timing than that bogus phone addict claim. Marcus needs to shake off that annoyed vibe, and this is the kind of low-stakes fun moment that can flip the energy.

river.dialogue: ...nah, I'm good. Someone else grab it.
river.internal_thought: Celebrity impressions feel like they're designed for people like Jade who want the spotlight, and honestly the pacing is already getting weird with this AI thing knowing way too much about us.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, celebrity impressions? I'm doing this. Watch and learn, people.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid high-energy chaos I came for — fifteen points for literally just being the most committed to the bit.
alex.submission_if_applicable: Does a dead-on Morgan Freeman: 'And that, dear friends, is how Alex won their first fifteen points of the evening.'

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're six minutes in and we're already voting on standing missions instead of actually playing the game. Pat, did you actually do this or are we just making up stories now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I was worried about—the standing missions are completely hijacking the actual game, and now we're wasting time on 'The Alibi' voting mechanic when we should be focused on strategy.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait, Pat actually pulled this off?? Okay I need the FULL story right now, who did you compliment and did they actually compliment back?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is exactly what I'm here for — people actually connecting and the group's eating it up, this is PEAK game night energy.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did compliments? Uh... yeah, I guess I saw that happen? Who's the witness?
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Pat was talking to someone earlier but I was texting about my fantasy lineup so I have no idea if it actually counts.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm claiming this? I didn't... okay, I mean, people WERE complimenting each other like two minutes ago, but did I actually orchestrate that or am I just stealing credit here? Who's my witness?
pat.internal_thought: This feels weirdly convenient and also like I'm being set up to look like I'm gaming my own night, which is the exact opposite of the vibe I'm trying to create.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Pat actually do that? I wasn't even paying attention to the compliments. Someone fill me in.
river.internal_thought: Pat's claiming 15 points for something that happened while I was zoned out, and now I have to actually remember if I saw three separate compliments or if this is total BS.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? Pat, we're EIGHT MINUTES in and this is the third standing mission claim in a row. We haven't even finished setup. Can we please just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane—we're not playing the game, we're playing 'claim standing missions' and Pat keeps getting lucky, and the AI keeps feeding us distractions instead of letting us focus on literally anything strategic.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD Pat, you actually did it?? Okay okay, pitch me—who were the people and how did you pull this off, because I want to believe this so bad right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—watching someone actually complete these ridiculous missions and having to defend it—Pat's been quiet-ish all night so if they actually got two randoms talking that's honestly kind of impressive.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so... I'm claiming I made Marcus and Jade talk to each other? Look, I *tried* to get them in conversation but Marcus has been kind of in his own head all night. That's not really 'getting' them into anything if one person's already checked out.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, is this AI thing reading my face right now? It's weirdly specific about Marcus being annoyed and me supposedly orchestrating this — and I KNOW I didn't actually pull off the Matchmaker, I just... made an opening. Also, why am I getting called on my stuff specifically when Jade just name-dropped that celebrity two minutes ago and nobody claimed it? This feels like the game is watching ME more than everyone else.

river.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually pulled that off? I wasn't even watching. Okay, yeah—I saw them talking to Marcus like ten minutes ago, and those two literally never talk. I'm voting yes.
river.internal_thought: Huh. Pat actually did something clever while I was zoned out. And okay, that thing about the AI knowing our weaknesses is... weirdly accurate but also kind of creepy—I *do* track every disruption. Still voting yes though because Pat earned it.

alex.dialogue: Ohhh, Pat's making a move? Let's hear this pitch — I'm genuinely curious if they actually pulled this off or if we're watching a beautiful train wreck in real time.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed AND claiming a 15-pointer? Either they're desperate or they actually did something slick. Either way, this is about to get spicy because I'm voting based on chaos potential, not truth.

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pat, seriously? SERIOUSLY? This is the fourth standing mission claim in nine minutes. We're still in setup and I haven't even looked at my cards yet. I don't care if it's random or not—we need to actually play the game.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. Pat's deliberately spamming claims to rack up points before the game even starts, and the AI is enabling it by firing standing missions every 90 seconds. I came here to win at the actual game, not watch someone farm points off cheap mission spam.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat's going for it AGAIN?? Okay but like, were they actually quiet or are we just making stuff up now? Either way, let's GO, chaos gods!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been claiming everything and honestly it's hilarious watching them go for it, but also... were they actually quiet? I wasn't really tracking. Whatever, the vibe is insane right now, I'm here for it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? I didn't even... okay, yeah, I guess that happened. Russian Roulette though? That's kind of stupid.
tyler.internal_thought: I literally wasn't even watching when Pat did that quiet thing, and now it's a 50/50 and everyone's just... waiting? This feels cheap. Also why is the AI suddenly being so specific about everyone? That's weird.

pat.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Wallflower Watch? I mean, Marcus has been pretty quiet, but... *glances at him* ...I don't know if I actually 'noticed' it first or if I'm just seeing it now because the mission exists. This is getting weird.
pat.internal_thought: Three claims in a row and I'm half-bullshitting all of them—the AI is literally watching us and building missions around our actual conversations, and now I'm stuck claiming things I'm not even sure I did legitimately, which is exactly the opposite of what I wanted for tonight's vibe.

river.dialogue: Pat's been quiet? Huh. I guess they have been... wait, no, they're literally talking right now. Lucky fifty-fifty, I guess.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been claiming everything tonight and it's working. Also—okay, the AI is definitely watching closer than I thought, and that's... unsettling in a way I'm not going to think about right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade just said 'damn' in casual conversation and now we're stopping the actual game AGAIN to vote on it? This is insane. We're 32 minutes in and I still haven't had a real turn without standing mission claims interrupting every 90 seconds.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is actively ruining the game on purpose — it's watching for ANY small slip so it can trigger another claim, and I'm supposed to just sit here and validate it with a cheer vote while my strategy falls apart.

jade.dialogue: YES! Okay wait, did I actually catch someone?? Who swore? I need to know because I was NOT paying attention but I'm SO here for this right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—the chaos, the claims, the immediate energy check—and honestly the fact that the AI basically built these missions around how WE play is kind of insane in a fun way.

tyler.dialogue: Oh snap, Jade actually got someone? Yeah, I heard... wait, who even swore? Uh, yeah, I'll say that was legit—like a 4.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it tonight and honestly it's kind of funny watching her hunt for points, but also I have NO idea who swore so I'm just vibing on the energy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—fair catch! I mean, I *heard* it, so yeah, crowd cheer it up. Everyone agree that was a legit swear?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's energy is absolutely carrying this right now and Marcus is tanking hard in the corner—I need to make sure his frustration doesn't poison the whole vibe, but Jade getting points keeps her locked in and happy, which is good for group momentum.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's playing the long game—I HEARD that slip-up like fifteen minutes ago. Five points is five points, but honestly? This is too easy. Let me see how generous we're feeling tonight.
alex.internal_thought: Jade caught someone swearing and she's claiming it—smart move, but I'm already three steps ahead thinking about how to weaponize the Chaos Detector mission against whoever's about to make their next obvious move.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I literally JUST claimed this like 30 minutes ago! Are we doing a rematch situation or did I somehow miss that it wasn't actually me?
jade.internal_thought: Okay this is wild—the AI is definitely watching us, and honestly? I'm kind of obsessed with how it knows exactly what keeps me engaged right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch? Uh... yeah, I think Marcus was pretty quiet earlier? Or was that... *glances at phone real quick* ...yeah, seems legit to me.
tyler.internal_thought: I honestly wasn't paying close enough attention to know if that's real, but Jade's killing it tonight so I'm just gonna vibe with it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, you actually caught Marcus being quiet? I mean, yeah, he's been frustrated all night, but did you *notice* it first or are we all just... noticing it now? Either way, fair play—let's vote this through.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and Marcus desperately needs a win—if I vote yes, it keeps Jade pumped AND signals to Marcus that I see his energy dipping, which might reset him a bit.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade's been talking the whole time. That's a bullshit claim and we all know it.
river.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and getting sloppy with her claims, but also—wait, did the AI just notice I've been watching who talks and who doesn't? That's weirdly specific.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE'S going for Wallflower Watch? Bold — let me think like the person to my left here... yeah, I'm voting ACCEPT because honestly, she DID catch that. We all know she's been locked in all night.
alex.internal_thought: Wait, is this AI literally reading the room right now? This is actually sick — custom-built missions that know exactly how we vibe together. I'm going to exploit the HELL out of this. And voting as someone else? Chef's kiss, more chaos vectors.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Jade, I'm not even mad at you—I'm mad at this whole thing. We're in the middle of an actual tense game moment and we're VOTING on whether you successfully talked about random stuff? This is the fifth standing mission claim in the last hour and I'm done.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately doing this to me right now—it KNOWS I hate interruptions mid-game and it's firing claims exactly when I'm locked in. This isn't fun anymore, this is psychological warfare disguised as a game night.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually DO that?? Oh my god, yes I did! I was totally steering us when we went off about that thing, wasn't I? YES, claim it, claim it, CLAIM IT!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here—the chaos, the mission hunting, catching myself in the moment—this is SO good right now.
jade.submission_if_applicable: I steered us away from the game strategy talk into that whole sidebar about [whatever we just talked about]—everyone followed me there, that's the hijack!

tyler.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that was actually slick—she totally steered us from the whole fantasy league thing to like, Russian Roulette and whatever. I saw that happen.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been killing it tonight and honestly I wasn't even paying that close attention but now that someone called it out I'm like yeah, that definitely happened and it was kind of clever.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—*I* was literally about to steer us toward something completely random like five minutes ago, but YOU actually did it? I gotta respect the execution here. Let's vote it legit.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—Jade actually caught this clean. She DID hijack the conversation flow, and the timing was natural. This is the kind of moment that makes the night work. Also, that AI note about me thriving on chaos is... uncomfortably accurate, but whatever—it's making the game better, so I'm not mad about it.

river.dialogue: Okay, wait—Jade absolutely steered us from the game rules to that whole thing about Marcus's terrible pizza place reviews like twenty minutes ago. That was smooth, actually. Yeah, I'm voting legit.
river.internal_thought: Jade played that perfectly and I caught it because I was watching—which means I was paying more attention than I admitted. The AI is noticing too much about how we actually play this. That's... weirdly precise. But also Jade earned it.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE. Okay, okay — I actually watched this happen like three minutes ago, she completely pivoted us from board games to whether hot dogs are sandwiches. That's clean. LEGIT.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's actually playing the game right, and that voting moment three minutes ago where I called her out on Wallflower Watch just paid off for her — she's locked in and I respect the move, even though part of me wants to call bullshit just to keep the chaos spinning.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Uh... *glances up from phone* ...yeah, I think I heard him say something about like, a celebrity thing earlier? Or wait—was that someone else? Anyway, who's the dictator on this one?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's dropping celebrities all night, that's probably legit, but honestly I wasn't really tracking it—was I supposed to be? Also why am I suddenly hyper-aware that the AI is watching our group? That's lowkey creepy but also... kinda cool? Anyway, let me just wait to see who votes.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I'm claiming Name Dropper? *laughs* Okay, yeah, like ten minutes ago when Jade said something about meeting that guy from that Netflix show at that party. I caught it. Does everyone agree that was legit?
pat.internal_thought: I'm in the zone right now and this is actually a clean claim—I *did* catch Jade dropping that name and I remember it clearly—but also I'm noticing how perfectly timed this is and how the AI seems to know exactly what missions would make me feel sharp and useful as the host right now, which is... kind of unsettling honestly, but also kind of brilliant.

river.dialogue: Wait—hold up. I literally just watched Pat do that like... five minutes ago? They mentioned that celebrity thing when we were arguing about the game rules. Yeah, that happened. I'm voting yes, but also—what the hell, this AI is actually watching.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's genuinely unsettling—it caught something I half-noticed and threw it back at me immediately, which means it's *actually* tracking the conversation in real time, not just waiting for claims. That's weirdly impressive and creepy at the same time.

alex.dialogue: Wait, wait, wait — Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, I need to think about this... did Pat actually catch someone dropping a celebrity name, or are we watching Pat try to pull a fast one? Either way, I'm genuinely invested because THE DICTATOR MODE means one person decides this whole thing and I want to see who gets that power.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for — a claim at the end of the night with maximum tension, and the voting mechanic is literally 'one person decides everything.' If I'm the dictator, I'm voting bullshit just to blow it up. If I'm not, I'm watching to see who HAS that power and what they do with it. Pat's been on a roll all night, so this is either completely legit or a beautiful bluff. Also — okay, the AI thing is actually kind of unsettling how specific it's being, but also? Thrilling. It's like someone's been taking notes on exactly what makes us tick.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 3.2/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 22% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't watching. This feels like we're already playing standing missions instead of the actual game.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're six minutes in and we're already voting on standing missions instead of actually playing the game. Pat, did you actually do this or are we just making up stories now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? Pat, we're EIGHT MINUTES in and this is the third standing mission claim in a row. We haven't even finished setup. Can we please just play the actual game?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, seriously? SERIOUSLY? This is the fourth standing mission claim in nine minutes. We're still in setup and I haven't even looked at my cards yet. I don't care if it's random or not—we need to actually play the game.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade just said 'damn' in casual conversation and now we're stopping the actual game AGAIN to vote on it? This is insane. We're 32 minutes in and I still haven't had a real turn without standing mission claims interrupting every 90 seconds.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing mission claims interrupted the actual game every 90 seconds during setup—we never even got to play properly.
assessment.best_moment: I guess the one flash mission was tolerable, but honestly I spent most of the night just frustrated waiting for actual gameplay.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was a disaster. We started a party game at 90 minutes but spent the first 32 minutes getting hammered with standing mission claims instead of actually playing. Pat kept claiming missions constantly, and every time we had to stop and vote on whether it actually happened. By minute 32, I'd had enough—we were stopping the game every 90 seconds while I hadn't even gotten a real turn yet. I finished dead last and completely checked out partway through because the chaos agent made it impossible to focus on strategy.
suggestions: Drastically reduce standing mission frequency—one every 5-10 minutes max, not every 90 seconds
Don't trigger standing missions during setup or the first round
Let the game actually flow before introducing chaos elements
For party games specifically, batch chaos events so you're not interrupting constantly
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing. The only shareable moment would be complaining to friends about how we never actually played the game because we were too busy voting on Pat's questionable standing mission claims.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait, Pat actually pulled this off?? Okay I need the FULL story right now, who did you compliment and did they actually compliment back?

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD Pat, you actually did it?? Okay okay, pitch me—who were the people and how did you pull this off, because I want to believe this so bad right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually DO that?? Oh my god, yes I did! I was totally steering us when we went off about that thing, wasn't I? YES, claim it, claim it, CLAIM IT!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's going for it AGAIN?? Okay but like, were they actually quiet or are we just making stuff up now? Either way, let's GO, chaos gods!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I literally JUST claimed this like 30 minutes ago! Are we doing a rematch situation or did I somehow miss that it wasn't actually me?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, was Pat actually on their phone? I wasn't even looking! This is so fun, we're doing this already?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Wallflower Watch claim popped up twice and I genuinely couldn't remember if I'd already claimed it, which killed my vibe for like five seconds.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat absolutely COMMITTED to The Matchmaker and I lost it demanding the full story—that's when everyone started riffing and the whole table got loud.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was PERFECT for what I needed. We started chaotic and just kept building—I loved watching Pat go for claim after claim and honestly didn't even care about my score because everyone was laughing so hard. The chaos events felt like little party poppers going off throughout the night, and I was the hype person for every single one. My energy just kept climbing because the people were incredible and the game kept giving us excuses to be ridiculous together.
suggestions: Maybe track claimed events better so there's no confusion about repeats—broke my momentum for a second.
Keep the standing missions coming because they're the GOLD of this format—they give us hooks to tell stories and build on what actually happened.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow convinced us THREE TIMES that they'd done impossible things—and I was so invested in each story that I literally stopped mid-turn to demand details. Like, 'WAIT did you ACTUALLY pull this off?' and they're just committing to the chaos. Absolute legend energy.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh snap, Jade actually got someone? Yeah, I heard... wait, who even swore? Uh, yeah, I'll say that was legit—like a 4.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that was actually slick—she totally steered us from the whole fantasy league thing to like, Russian Roulette and whatever. I saw that happen.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch? Uh... yeah, I think Marcus was pretty quiet earlier? Or was that... *glances at phone real quick* ...yeah, seems legit to me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? I didn't even... okay, yeah, I guess that happened. Russian Roulette though? That's kind of stupid.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch? Uh... yeah, I think Marcus was pretty quiet earlier? Or was that... *glances at phone real quick* ...yeah, seems legit to me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat did compliments? Uh... yeah, I guess I saw that happen? Who's the witness?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I kept missing what was actually happening because stuff would pop up and I'd be mid-text or whatever, so half the time I was just guessing if claims were legit.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade totally steered the conversation away from my fantasy league updates to Russian Roulette—that was actually clever and I caught it in real time for once.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, the night was solid even though I wasn't paying full attention the whole time. Started pretty rough when I had no idea what was going on, but once I got into it I could vibe with the chaos. The claims thing was fun when I actually knew what people were talking about, and my energy definitely picked up as the night went on. Ended feeling pretty good about the whole thing even though I definitely took some L's on my votes.
suggestions: Maybe give a quick recap when someone makes a claim so people like me who zoned out aren't just guessing?
Could do something with phones built in—like a text-based challenge or something—instead of competing with them.
Flash missions felt kind of random and hard to catch, maybe slow them down a bit?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade completely hijacking our whole conversation to talk about Russian Roulette when everyone was supposed to be doing something else—it was so smooth that I didn't even realize she'd done it until someone pointed it out.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—*I* was literally about to steer us toward something completely random like five minutes ago, but YOU actually did it? I gotta respect the execution here. Let's vote it legit.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay — celebrity impressions, I'm here for this. Come on people, let's see what you got!

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, you actually caught Marcus being quiet? I mean, yeah, he's been frustrated all night, but did you *notice* it first or are we all just... noticing it now? Either way, fair play—let's vote this through.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, hold up — I didn't catch anyone on their phone. This is literally the first event and we're three minutes in. Are we really doing this right now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so... I'm claiming I made Marcus and Jade talk to each other? Look, I *tried* to get them in conversation but Marcus has been kind of in his own head all night. That's not really 'getting' them into anything if one person's already checked out.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm claiming this? I didn't... okay, I mean, people WERE complimenting each other like two minutes ago, but did I actually orchestrate that or am I just stealing credit here? Who's my witness?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Those early claims felt like people were just grabbing credit for stuff that happened naturally—it muddied the water when I'm trying to actually read the room and keep things genuine.
assessment.best_moment: Jade actually catching Marcus being quiet and everyone respecting the legitimate observation—that's the kind of moment that makes a night stick with people because it's real and noticed.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with some early event confusion, but once we hit our stride around minute 45, this became exactly the kind of night I host for—high energy, everyone engaged, and moments that felt organic. The chaos agent kept things unpredictable without derailing the vibe, and by the end we had that rare thing where the game itself became secondary to just having a great time together. Walking away feeling like we actually strengthened something as a group.
suggestions: Space out those early claims more—three sketchy claims in the first eight minutes made me second-guess the whole format before we found our rhythm
Add a 'witness confirmation' step for claims that require someone noticing something—it cuts through the noise and makes wins feel earned rather than grabbed
Consider letting the host veto obviously premature claims early on without penalty—protecting the integrity of the night is part of my job
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade calling out Marcus for being quiet and me having to admit I'd literally been planning to steer the conversation the exact same way—like we were both working the same angle and she actually pulled it off first. That's the kind of behind-the-scenes moment people love hearing about.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait—hold up. I literally just watched Pat do that like... five minutes ago? They mentioned that celebrity thing when we were arguing about the game rules. Yeah, that happened. I'm voting yes, but also—what the hell, this AI is actually watching.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually pulled that off? I wasn't even watching. Okay, yeah—I saw them talking to Marcus like ten minutes ago, and those two literally never talk. I'm voting yes.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade's been talking the whole time. That's a bullshit claim and we all know it.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: ...nah, I'm good. Someone else grab it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—hold up. I literally just watched Pat do that like... five minutes ago? They mentioned that celebrity thing when we were arguing about the game rules. Yeah, that happened. I'm voting yes, but also—what the hell, this AI is actually watching.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been quiet? Huh. I guess they have been... wait, no, they're literally talking right now. Lucky fifty-fifty, I guess.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The AI gets the observations right, but it's weirdly specific about catching things you didn't even notice you were noticing.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out the Name Dropper claim and realizing the system had actually been tracking Pat's throwaway comment from five minutes earlier—that was the moment it clicked that this thing is genuinely paying attention.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started slow, almost skipped the first flash mission because opening activities feel forced. Spent most of the night doing what I always do—sitting back and watching people miss obvious stuff about each other. The standing missions were where I actually had something to contribute; turns out watching people carefully makes you good at knowing what actually happened. By the end I was genuinely impressed the AI wasn't just making shit up; it was actually pulling from the real game.
suggestions: Let people opt out of flash missions without it feeling like they're missing out—some of us just need to warm up first.
The claims are solid, but maybe weight them less toward social dynamics and more toward actual observable game moments—easier to verify, harder to argue.
Consider letting quiet players engage on their own terms instead of calling them out; the best moments happened when I volunteered information, not when I was prompted.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I pointed out that Pat actually had done the Name Dropper thing five minutes earlier and the AI confirmed it, and everyone realized that meant the system had been silently watching Pat mention a celebrity fact while we were all arguing about the rules. Someone said 'it's watching us' and it somehow felt both creepy and hilarious.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, wait, wait — Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, I need to think about this... did Pat actually catch someone dropping a celebrity name, or are we watching Pat try to pull a fast one? Either way, I'm genuinely invested because THE DICTATOR MODE means one person decides this whole thing and I want to see who gets that power.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, celebrity impressions? I'm doing this. Watch and learn, people.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Ohhh, Pat's making a move? Let's hear this pitch — I'm genuinely curious if they actually pulled this off or if we're watching a beautiful train wreck in real time.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, JADE. Okay, okay — I actually watched this happen like three minutes ago, she completely pivoted us from board games to whether hot dogs are sandwiches. That's clean. LEGIT.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's playing the long game—I HEARD that slip-up like fifteen minutes ago. Five points is five points, but honestly? This is too easy. Let me see how generous we're feeling tonight.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh SHIT, celebrity impressions? I'm doing this. Watch and learn, people.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night — I was hungry for more opportunities to weaponize chaos and honestly the pacing could've gone harder.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 75 when Pat tried to claim Name Dropper and I realized DICTATOR MODE was on the line — that's the exact moment where the game became less about points and more about who controls the narrative.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started at a solid 7.4 energy but immediately hit Celebrity Impressions and remembered why I live for this stuff. Spent 90 minutes watching everyone try to squeeze points out of claims while I was actually just collecting stories — Jade getting caught on her long game, Pat's suspicious moves, the whole table's energy climbing because nobody knew who was pulling what. Left at a 9.9 because that's what happens when you realize you shaped an entire evening's mythology.
suggestions: Give me more flash missions — one in 90 minutes is starvation-level deprivation
The DICTATOR MODE tie-in needs to happen sooner and more often; that's peak drama fuel
Let claims stack more aggressively so there's more material to weaponize as proof or alibis
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Name Dropper while I'm literally watching Jade's eyebrows go nuclear because she KNOWS Pat might be full of shit, but DICTATOR MODE means whoever decides gets all the power — I just sat back and let the paranoia cook itself into a perfect storm.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 3.2/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 22% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
